title: Impairment of perceptual metacognitive accuracy and reduced prefrontal grey matter volume in first-episode psychosis.

assets.abstract: INTRODUCTION: Metacognition, or "thinking about thinking", is a higher-order thought process that allows for the evaluation of perceptual processes for accuracy. Metacognitive accuracy is associated with the grey matter volume (GMV) in the prefrontal cortex (PFC), an area also impacted in schizophrenia. The present study set out to investigate whether deficits in metacognitive accuracy are present in the early stages of psychosis. METHODS: Metacognitive accuracy in first-episode psychosis (FEP)

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
